question: Is the statement supported by the evidence?
answer: Summary - Most likely cause: the printer assignment Group Policy (GPO) for location-based printers was not applied to the new user account (so no printers are mapped). This pattern was resolved by forcing GPO processing in a recent, identical case
context: SOURCE 39: ﻿ Ticket summary: Ticket name: KF60: Printer not visible in system Ticket number: TI-1621089 Company: Customer Company 60 AG Contact person: Contact Person 08 Owner: Employee 15 Origin: Email Type: Incident Created on: 22.10.25 08:44 Closure Closure date: 22.10.25 09:27 Closure text: GPO not applied. Group policies updated. Printer visible again. Activities 22.10.25 08:30 – Email from Contact Person 08 Good morning A new employee does not see any printer displayed in the system. Colleagues in the same office see the printer normally. Could you please check this? Kind regards 22.10.25 08:44 Ticket created and assigned to Employee 15. 22.10.25 09:00 – Analysis User account checked. User is a member of the correct AD group for location-based printers. Group Policy status checked → GPO for printer assignment not applied. gpupdate /force executed and user logged in again. Printer is then automatically mapped. Test print successful. 22.10.25 09:20 – Customer feedback Printer is now visible and working. 22.10.25 09:27 – Ticket closure Dear Contact Person 08 The printer assignment was not correctly applied due to a Group Policy that had not been processed. After updating the Group Policies, the printer is visible again and fully functional. Kind regards Consulting Sample Company SOURCE 41: ﻿general info & description Print & Scanning Infrastructure Standard – Customer Company Version: 1.0 Date: 15.10.2025 Author: IT Infrastructure Status: Approved This document describes the standard for the print and scan infrastructure within the customer company. The objective is a stable, centrally managed printing environment for all locations based on the following core principles: • Central printer management via print servers • Automatic printer assignment via Group Policy (GPO) • Standardized driver versions • Location-based printer concepts • Uniform configuration for Scan-to-Mail The print infrastructure is based on: • Windows print servers • Location-based printer queues • User- or group-based assignment • VLAN-separated printer networks Manual installation of local printers is not intended. design Architecture overview User device → Print server → Printer (in printer VLAN) Printers are not directly accessible from the client but are addressed exclusively via the central print server. Printer provisioning Printers are: • Created as a queue on the print server • Configured with a standardized driver • Assigned via GPO based on location or department Default printers are set automatically. Driver standard Only approved and tested driver versions are used. After Windows updates, it must be verified: • Whether drivers remain compatible • Whether Point-and-Print restrictions are active • Whether printers are still mapped correctly Scan-to-Mail Scan-to-Mail is performed: • Via a dedicated SMTP relay • Using a configured service account • Secured with TLS Changes to mail servers or authentication mechanisms may affect scan functionality. Location concept Each location has: • At least one default printer • One multifunction printer with scan capability • Assignment via AD groups Printers are located in a separate printer VLAN. decision tree & use cases Decision Tree Initial question: Does the issue occur when printing or when scanning? A) Printing affected A1: Is the printer visible in Windows Device Manager or in “Printers & Scanners”? • No → Check whether the user is a member of the correct AD group. If yes → Check whether the GPO has been applied. If GPO not applied → Perform Group Policy update. If GPO applied → Check reachability of the print server. • Yes → Proceed to A2. A2: Is the print job displayed in the queue? • No → Issue is likely client-side (check driver or assignment). • Yes → Proceed to A3. A3: Does the print job remain stuck in the queue? • Yes → Check whether the queue is paused. If not → Check the Print Spooler service. If Spooler is running → Check network connection to the printer (Ping/VLAN). • No → Proceed to A4. A4: Does the issue affect only one location? • Yes → Check VLAN reachability. Check DNS resolution of the printer. Check location-based GPO assignment. • No → Check driver compatibility or print server service. A5: Did the issue occur after a Windows update? • Yes → Check driver compatibility. Check Point-and-Print policy. Validate security policies. • No → Further analysis according to standard process. B) Scanning affected B1: Is printing still working? • Yes → Issue likely in Scan-to-Mail or destination path. • No → First analyze printing issue according to section A. B2: Is an error message displayed during scanning? • SMTP or connection error → Check SMTP relay reachability. Check service account authentication credentials. Validate TLS configuration. • No error message, but no delivery → Check mail queue. Check spam/filter rules.

SOURCE 2: ﻿ Ticket summary: Ticket name: KF60: Incorrect printer automatically set as default Ticket number: TI-1623017 Company: Customer Company 60 AG Contact person: Contact Person 18 Owner: Employee 23 Origin: Email Type: Incident Created on: 18.11.25 08:49 Closure Closure date: 24.11.25 15:11 Closure text: Printer assignment and default configuration corrected according to Print & Scanning Infrastructure Standard. Behavior stable. Activities 18.11.25 08:35 – Email from Contact Person 18 Good morning Several employees in the Finance department report that their default printer automatically switches to “MFP-Zuerich-01”, although they work at the Basel location. They must manually select the correct printer before each print job. The issue seems to have started last week. Kind regards 18.11.25 08:49 Ticket created and assigned to Employee 23. 18.11.25 09:20 – Initial analysis Remote connection established with an affected user. Default printer is indeed “MFP-Zuerich-01”. Both Basel and Zurich printers are visible in the printer list. Comparison with a user from another department shows the correct location printer set. 18.11.25 10:05 – Inquiry to customer Good day Were there any recent organizational changes or group adjustments in the Finance department? Kind regards 18.11.25 10:40 – Customer response Yes, the Finance team was moved into a new AD group last week. The old group was deactivated. 19.11.25 09:10 – GPO assignment analysis Comparison with “Print & Scanning Infrastructure Standard – Version 1.0”. In the section Printer Provisioning it is defined that: • Printer assignments are location-based via AD groups • Default printers per location are set via GPO • No parallel groups with identical function may exist Review shows that the new Finance group is additionally a member of a higher-level Zurich location group. As a result, the Zurich printer is also mapped via GPO and set as default. 19.11.25 11:30 – Test Temporary removal of the incorrect group membership. gpupdate /force executed. User logged in again. Default printer now correctly remains “MFP-Basel-01”. 20.11.25 – Coordination with customer Confirmation that the group structure had not been fully cleaned up during the reorganization. Correction performed according to blueprint specification. 24.11.25 14:50 – Customer feedback Multiple users tested. Default printer remains correct. No further automatic switching. 24.11.25 15:11 – Ticket closure Dear Contact Person 18 The root cause was an inconsistent group membership following an organizational adjustment. Printer assignment was corrected according to the “Print & Scanning Infrastructure Standard”. The correct location printer now remains permanently set as default. Kind regards Consulting Sample Company

SOURCE 22: ﻿ Ticket summary: Ticket name: KF60: Location printer Basel not responding to print jobs Ticket number: TI-1622483 Company: Customer Company 60 AG Contact person: Contact Person 15 Owner: Employee 21 Origin: Email Type: Incident Created on: 06.11.25 08:37 Closure Closure date: 12.11.25 14:52 Closure text: Network and queue configuration adjusted according to Print & Scanning Infrastructure Standard. Function restored. Activities 06.11.25 08:25 – Email from Contact Person 15 Good morning Since yesterday, the printer “MFP-Basel-01” is no longer responding to print jobs. The documents remain in the queue, and in some cases disappear without being printed. Other printers in the building are functioning normally. Could you please check this? Kind regards 06.11.25 08:37 Ticket created and assigned to Employee 21. 06.11.25 09:10 – Initial analysis Remote access to an affected user. Print job briefly appears in the print server queue, then disappears without printing. Queue itself is not paused. Print Spooler service is running. Test print directly from the print server to the printer also fails. 06.11.25 10:05 – Inquiry to customer Good day Were there any recent changes to the network or infrastructure at the Basel location? Thank you for your brief feedback. 06.11.25 11:20 – Customer response Last week, a new network segment for devices was introduced. The printers were moved into a separate VLAN. No other changes are known to us. 07.11.25 09:00 – Further analysis Ping from print server to printer unsuccessful. Ping from a client within the same location network successful. This indicates segmentation between the server network and the printer VLAN. Comparison with document “Print & Scanning Infrastructure Standard – Version 1.0”. In the section Location Concept, it is defined that printers operate in a separate VLAN but must be reachable from the print server. Routing and firewall rules must be configured accordingly. 07.11.25 11:30 – Internal coordination with Network Team Confirmation that the new firewall rule did not allow the server network access to the printer VLAN. Rule adjusted according to standard. 10.11.25 – Test phase Printing from print server successful. Multiple test prints from user workstations performed. No abnormalities observed. 12.11.25 14:40 – Customer feedback Printer is functioning normally again. Larger print jobs are also processed correctly. 12.11.25 14:52 – Ticket closure Dear Contact Person 15 The root cause was a missing network allowance between the print server and the newly configured printer VLAN at the Basel location. The configuration was adjusted according to the “Print & Scanning Infrastructure Standard”. The printer is fully reachable again. Kind regards Consulting Sample Company

Printer provisioning Printers are: • Created as a queue on the print server • Configured with a standardized driver • Assigned via GPO based on location or department Default printers are set automatically. Driver standard Only approved and tested driver versions are used. After Windows updates, it must be verified: • Whether drivers remain compatible • Whether Point-and-Print restrictions are active • Whether printers are still mapped correctly Scan-to-Mail Scan-to-Mail is performed: • Via a dedicated SMTP relay • Using a configured service account • Secured with TLS Changes to mail servers or authentication mechanisms may affect scan functionality. Location concept Each location has: • At least one default printer • One multifunction printer with scan capability • Assignment via AD groups Printers are located in a separate printer VLAN. decision tree & use cases Decision Tree Initial question: Does the issue occur when printing or when scanning? A) Printing affected A1: Is the printer visible in Windows Device Manager or in “Printers & Scanners”? • No → Check whether the user is a member of the correct AD group. If yes → Check whether the GPO has been applied. If GPO not applied → Perform Group Policy update. If GPO applied → Check reachability of the print server. • Yes → Proceed to A2. A2: Is the print job displayed in the queue? • No → Issue is likely client-side (check driver or assignment). • Yes → Proceed to A3. A3: Does the print job remain stuck in the queue? • Yes → Check whether the queue is paused. If not → Check the Print Spooler service. If Spooler is running → Check network connection to the printer (Ping/VLAN). • No → Proceed to A4. A4: Does the issue affect only one location? • Yes → Check VLAN reachability. Check DNS resolution of the printer. Check location-based GPO assignment. • No → Check driver compatibility or print server service. A5: Did the issue occur after a Windows update? • Yes → Check driver compatibility. Check Point-and-Print policy. Validate security policies. • No → Further analysis according to standard process. B) Scanning affected B1: Is printing still working? • Yes → Issue likely in Scan-to-Mail or destination path. • No → First analyze printing issue according to section A. B2: Is an error message displayed during scanning? • SMTP or connection error → Check SMTP relay reachability. Check service account authentication credentials. Validate TLS configuration. • No error message, but no delivery → Check mail queue. Check spam/filter rules.

question: Is the statement supported by the evidence?
answer: . - The GPO is present but there is evidence of print server or server‑side problems (e.g., print server services, driver compatibility or after a Windows update) — such issues required deeper infrastructure work in other incidents

answer: . Recommendation on ticket classification - Open this as a 1st‑level incident ticket and perform steps 1–3 above. If those steps do not restore printers and you identify server reachability, VLAN, or print server problems, escalate to 2nd‑level/Network (include test results and whether gpupdate /force changed anything)
context: SOURCE 39: ﻿ Ticket summary: Ticket name: KF60: Printer not visible in system Ticket number: TI-1621089 Company: Customer Company 60 AG Contact person: Contact Person 08 Owner: Employee 15 Origin: Email Type: Incident Created on: 22.10.25 08:44 Closure Closure date: 22.10.25 09:27 Closure text: GPO not applied. Group policies updated. Printer visible again. Activities 22.10.25 08:30 – Email from Contact Person 08 Good morning A new employee does not see any printer displayed in the system. Colleagues in the same office see the printer normally. Could you please check this? Kind regards 22.10.25 08:44 Ticket created and assigned to Employee 15. 22.10.25 09:00 – Analysis User account checked. User is a member of the correct AD group for location-based printers. Group Policy status checked → GPO for printer assignment not applied. gpupdate /force executed and user logged in again. Printer is then automatically mapped. Test print successful. 22.10.25 09:20 – Customer feedback Printer is now visible and working. 22.10.25 09:27 – Ticket closure Dear Contact Person 08 The printer assignment was not correctly applied due to a Group Policy that had not been processed. After updating the Group Policies, the printer is visible again and fully functional. Kind regards Consulting Sample Company SOURCE 22: ﻿ Ticket summary: Ticket name: KF60: Location printer Basel not responding to print jobs Ticket number: TI-1622483 Company: Customer Company 60 AG Contact person: Contact Person 15 Owner: Employee 21 Origin: Email Type: Incident Created on: 06.11.25 08:37 Closure Closure date: 12.11.25 14:52 Closure text: Network and queue configuration adjusted according to Print & Scanning Infrastructure Standard. Function restored. Activities 06.11.25 08:25 – Email from Contact Person 15 Good morning Since yesterday, the printer “MFP-Basel-01” is no longer responding to print jobs. The documents remain in the queue, and in some cases disappear without being printed. Other printers in the building are functioning normally. Could you please check this? Kind regards 06.11.25 08:37 Ticket created and assigned to Employee 21. 06.11.25 09:10 – Initial analysis Remote access to an affected user. Print job briefly appears in the print server queue, then disappears without printing. Queue itself is not paused. Print Spooler service is running. Test print directly from the print server to the printer also fails. 06.11.25 10:05 – Inquiry to customer Good day Were there any recent changes to the network or infrastructure at the Basel location? Thank you for your brief feedback. 06.11.25 11:20 – Customer response Last week, a new network segment for devices was introduced. The printers were moved into a separate VLAN. No other changes are known to us. 07.11.25 09:00 – Further analysis Ping from print server to printer unsuccessful. Ping from a client within the same location network successful. This indicates segmentation between the server network and the printer VLAN. Comparison with document “Print & Scanning Infrastructure Standard – Version 1.0”. In the section Location Concept, it is defined that printers operate in a separate VLAN but must be reachable from the print server. Routing and firewall rules must be configured accordingly. 07.11.25 11:30 – Internal coordination with Network Team Confirmation that the new firewall rule did not allow the server network access to the printer VLAN. Rule adjusted according to standard. 10.11.25 – Test phase Printing from print server successful. Multiple test prints from user workstations performed. No abnormalities observed. 12.11.25 14:40 – Customer feedback Printer is functioning normally again. Larger print jobs are also processed correctly. 12.11.25 14:52 – Ticket closure Dear Contact Person 15 The root cause was a missing network allowance between the print server and the newly configured printer VLAN at the Basel location. The configuration was adjusted according to the “Print & Scanning Infrastructure Standard”. The printer is fully reachable again.
